body:
README excerpt:
# Kage ### Shared repo memory for AI coding agents Kage gives Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, and other MCP agents the repo context they keep forgetting: commands, decisions, bugs, conventions, code paths, symbols, tests, and teammate knowledge.

Website · Live viewer · npm · Quick start · How it works

![K

context.fact: Generated repo overview summarizes package metadata and the README as a navigation aid for agent startup.
context.why: Agents need fast repo orientation before deeper recall or code graph queries, but generated overview memory should stay separate from human rationale.
context.trigger: Recall when an agent needs first-pass repo purpose, scripts, stack, or README context.
context.action: Use this as orientation only, then inspect source-backed memory and code graph facts for implementation decisions.
context.verification: Generated from package.json and README.md when present.
context.risk_if_forgotten: Agents may waste context rediscovering basic repo purpose or treat generated overview text as deeper semantic memory.
context.stale_when: package.json or README.md changes enough that the generated overview no longer matches the repo.
